tagline: A quiet week on the commit front, a loud one on the secrets front.
editionNote: Zero releases, two repos ticking over, and one API key that briefly saw the light of day.

articles.headline: AI agent helpfully publishes API key to GitHub for the whole class to see
articles.deck: A pgMustard API key hitched a ride in a PR comment when an AI agent dumped raw testing output during rpg v0.8.0 development.
articles.body: While testing the new \explain share pgmustard feature in rpg v0.8.0, an AI agent posted its testing output — API key included — straight into a GitHub PR comment. @NikolayS spotted the exposure quickly and rotated the key before any damage was done. The lesson, as always: agents will do exactly what you let them do, and they have no concept of embarrassment. Treat AI-generated output in public repos the way you treat a toddler with a microphone — assume the worst will be broadcast.
articles.illustrationPrompt: robot accidentally holding up a secret key on a stage in front of an audience

articles.headline: Seventy-two commits land in the postgres mirror, none of them news
articles.deck: The official PostgreSQL mirror keeps humming along with a healthy week of upstream activity.
articles.body: @NikolayS's mirror of the official PostgreSQL repository logged 72 commits this week. As ever, this is a read-only mirror — patches go through the mailing list, not pull requests. Steady upstream pulse, nothing more to report.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a mirror reflecting a busy workshop with seventy-two small gears turning

articles.headline: pg_pipe gets five quiet commits and stays under the radar
articles.deck: The pure-SQL incremental pipeline tool sees a small burst of activity with no fanfare.
articles.body: @NikolayS pushed 5 commits to pg_pipe this week. No releases, no open PRs, no issues filed — just steady, heads-down work on the anti-extension pipeline framework. pg_pipe remains pure SQL and PL/pgSQL, targeting Postgres 14+ across managed services.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a small pipe quietly carrying data drops through a PostgreSQL elephant silhouette

closingNote: No releases were harmed in the making of this edition. One API key was.
